title: hack/man_page_checker - improve diagnostics
body:
Make the errors more readable, with clearer instructions on
what to look for, and which filename, and what we expect to
see, and perhaps even how to approach a fix.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body:
Sample output from current network-create pr:
```
Inconsistent subcommand descriptions:
  podman-network-create.1.md       = 'Create a Podman CNI network'
  podman-network.1.md              = 'Create a CNI network for Podman'
Please ensure that the NAME section of podman-network-create.1.md
matches the subcommand description in podman-network.1.md
```
Sample output from files hand-tweaked to produce errors:
```
Inconsistent capitalization in SYNOPSIS in podman-network-create.1.md
  '**podman network creat**  [*options*] NAME' should not contain upper-case characters

Inconsistent program name in SYNOPSIS in podman-volume-create.1.md:
  SYNOPSIS = podman volumes create (expected: 'podman volume create')

Inconsistent program NAME in podman-volume-create.1.md:
  NAME= podman-volume-creat  (expected: podman-volume-create)
```

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
